title: How CITRA Associates Approaches Every New Project
author: CITRA Associates
metaDescription: Discover how CITRA Associates approaches every new project — from the first client conversation through design, approvals, construction supervision, and handover.
excerpt: Every CITRA Associates project begins with listening before drawing. This article explains our six-stage process — from discovery and brief development through schematic design, statutory approvals, working drawings, construction supervision, and final handover — and what clients can expect at each stage.

faqs.question: How long does a typical CITRA Associates project take from first meeting to completion?
faqs.answer: A typical residential project with CITRA Associates — from initial consultation through design, approvals, construction, and handover — takes approximately 24 to 36 months depending on size, complexity, and the speed of the approval process. Commercial projects may have tighter timelines when client occupancy requirements drive the schedule. We provide detailed programme schedules at the outset of every project.

faqs.question: Does CITRA Associates manage the construction process or only the design?
faqs.answer: CITRA Associates provides full architectural services including construction supervision and project management as part of our standard engagement. We conduct regular site visits, review contractor submissions, certify payment applications, and manage the quality control process through to completion certificate. This end-to-end involvement is what ensures the built result matches the design intent.

faqs.question: How involved does the client need to be during the design process?
faqs.answer: Client involvement is essential particularly at key decision points — brief finalisation, concept approval, schematic design review, material selections, and working drawing approvals. We structure the process so these decision points are clear and well-prepared, minimising the time clients need to spend while ensuring they have full ownership of every significant design choice.
